summary: Comprehensive testing of Sales Order commitment reconciliation feature (iteration 59). All 14 backend tests passed covering: decrease qty creates order_release, increase qty creates order_commitment, unchanged qty creates no movement (action=none), multi-line reconciliation, cancel releases all remaining net commitments, repeat cancel is idempotent (adjustments=0), negative qty returns 422, non-existent SO returns 422, derive_balances committed recalculates correctly, available = on_hand + incoming - committed formula verified. Frontend tests verified: 'Cancel & Release Inventory' button visible on existing SO panel (data-testid='bc-so-cancel-order-btn'), Inventory Ledger page renders with workspaces, Balances tab shows items with committed/available values, Incoming tab shows records with status badges and action buttons.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order — decrease line qty creates order_release movement (100→70 releases 30)
Backend: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order — increase line qty creates additional order_commitment (100→150 commits 50)
Backend: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order — unchanged qty creates no movement (action=none, delta=0)
Backend: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order — multi-line reconciliation (item1 decrease + item2 increase simultaneously)
Backend: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order — cancel releases all remaining net commitments (2 adjustments)
Backend: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order — repeat cancel is idempotent (adjustments=0 on second call)
Backend: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order — negative qty returns HTTP 422 with 'Negative' in message
Backend: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order — non-existent SO returns HTTP 422 with 'no order_commitment' message
Backend: derive_balances — committed recalculates correctly after reconciliation adjustments
Backend: derive_balances — available = on_hand + incoming - committed verified after edit and cancel
REGRESSION: POST /api/inventory-ledger/release — still works (released 30 units)
REGRESSION: POST /api/incoming-supply/from-shortage — still works (created supply record)
REGRESSION: POST /api/incoming-supply/{id}/status — transitions still work (planned→ordered)
REGRESSION: GET /api/inventory-ledger/customers — still lists workspaces correctly
Frontend: Login with admin/admin works correctly
Frontend: Navigate to Sales Orders page shows 6 documents with 'Already Created' status badges
Frontend: Click View on document with existing SO opens Document Detail
Frontend: 'Cancel & Release Inventory' button found with data-testid='bc-so-cancel-order-btn'
Frontend: Button text is ' Cancel & Release Inventory' and is visible
Frontend: BC Sales Order panel (data-testid='bc-sales-order-panel') found and visible
Frontend: Inventory Ledger page renders with 11 customer workspace cards
Frontend: Hormel Foods workspace shows summary: 7 Items Tracked, 2,160 On Hand, 300 Incoming, 6,324 Committed, 3 Shortages
Frontend: Balances tab shows items with columns: ITEM, WAREHOUSE, OWNERSHIP, ON HAND, INCOMING, COMMITTED, AVAILABLE, UOM, STATUS
Frontend: Incoming tab shows records with columns: ITEM, WAREHOUSE, QTY, UOM, ETA, SOURCE, STATUS, ACTIONS
Frontend: Incoming tab shows status badges (received=green, expected=blue) and action buttons
critical_code_review_comments: reconcile_sales_order function in inventory_so_integration.py correctly implements delta logic: increase creates order_commitment, decrease creates order_release
_get_net_committed helper correctly calculates outstanding = abs(commitments) - abs(releases)
Idempotent cancel behavior: when net_committed is 0, creates no movements (action=none)
Per-line results include all required fields: item, previous_committed, new_qty, delta, action
Frontend handleCancelOrder callback correctly calls reconcileSalesOrder with cancelled=true
Button data-testid='bc-so-cancel-order-btn' properly set for testing automation
seed_data_used: Each test creates unique workspace with UUID prefix (TEST_RECONCILE_*, TEST_REGRESSION_*). Items seeded with opening_balance movements, then order_commitment movements created. Workspace deactivated on teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SO reconciliation feature fully tested. Key endpoint: POST /api/inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order with {sales_order_id, lines: [{item, qty}], cancelled: bool}. For edits: compares new qty to current net committed (commitments - releases) and creates delta movements. For cancel: releases ALL remaining net commitments for all items on the SO. Idempotent cancel: repeat call creates no movements if net already 0. Frontend button data-testid='bc-so-cancel-order-btn' visible on existing SO panel. reconcileSalesOrder API function added to frontend api.js calling /inventory-ledger/reconcile-sales-order.

request_model.lines: [{item: string, qty: float}] (new quantities per line)

actions.commit: created when new_qty > previous_committed (order_commitment with negative delta)
actions.release: created when new_qty < previous_committed (order_release with negative delta)

cancel_button.location: BC Sales Order Created panel (existingSO display)

inventory_ledger_page.workspaces: Customer cards with name, code, negative_balance_policy
inventory_ledger_page.balances_tab: Items with On Hand, Incoming, Committed, Available, Status
inventory_ledger_page.incoming_tab: Supply records with status badges and action buttons
